abstract: This article explores the transformative impact of the Circle of Concerned African Women Theologians in gender-mainstreaming higher theological education across the continent. Grounded in the framework of African Women’s Theology, the study analyzes the organization’s initiatives in championing gender equality, inclusivity, and intersectional solidarity within the academic sphere. It highlights the Circle’s historical journey in promoting gender-sensitive curricula and mentoring a new generation of African women scholars. Employing a qualitative research design, the study utilizes African Women’s Theology as an interpretive lens to evaluate the significant strides made by the Circle toward dismantling patriarchal structures and fostering a more equitable theological landscape in Africa.
